wellbeing.headline: Common antidepressant may ease long COVID’s crushing fatigue

wellbeing.headline: Popular sugar substitutes linked to faster brain aging
wellbeing.summary: Several popular sugar substitutes may not be as harmless as they seem. Adults who consumed the most artificial sweeteners showed substantially faster declines in memory and thinking, especially if they were under 60 or had diabetes. The highest intake was linked to cognitive aging roughly 1.6 years faster than the lowest intake. Researchers stressed that more studies are needed before concluding that sweeteners are the cause.

wellbeing.headline: Mediterranean diet may activate tiny proteins that protect the heart and brain
wellbeing.summary: The Mediterranean diet may influence aging through tiny proteins produced inside the cell’s mitochondria. Older adults who followed the diet most closely had higher levels of humanin and SHMOOSE, which have been linked to heart and brain protection. Olive oil, fish, legumes, and fewer refined carbohydrates appeared especially important. The findings could eventually help scientists develop more personalized nutrition plans…
